questions.questionText: Skin: Skin Issues (Other skin issue description)

responseOptions.responseText: Stable: previously deteriorating wound characteristics plateaued
Stalled: previously improving wound characteristics plateaued.
Monitoring: Wound healed or closed with continued monitoring.
Resolved: Wound healed and / or closed.
Stage 1 Pressure Ulcer / Injury: Non-blanchable erythema of intact skin
Stage 2 Pressure Ulcer / Injury: Partial-thickness skin loss with exposed dermis
Stage 3 Pressure Ulcer / Injury: Full-thickness skin loss
Stage 4 Pressure Ulcer / Injury: Full-thickness skin and tissue loss
Unstageable pressure injuries presenting as deep tissue injury

responseOptions.responseText: Purulent: indication of pus, typically thick, yellow, green, tan or brown
Seropurulent: mixture of purulent and serous, usually watery, yellow, green, tan or brown
Serous: clear watery fluid, which is separated from solid elements
Sanguineous:  Indicates active bleeding, typically bright red
Serosanguineous: mixture of serous and sanguineous fluid, typically pale, red, and watery

questions.questionText: Skin: Skin Issues (Exudate - odor after cleansing)
Skin: Skin Issues (Other wound bed information)

responseOptions.responseText: Attached: Edge appears flush with wound bed or as a sloping edge
Non-attached: Edge appears as a cliff
Rolled Edge (Epibole): Edge appears curled under
Epithelialization: New, pink to purple, shiny skin tissue
Denuded: Loss of epidermis caused by exposure to urine, feces, body fluids, wound exudate or friction.
Erythema: Redness of the skin - may be intense bright red to dark red or purple
Fragile: Skin that is at risk for breakdown

questions.questionText: Skin: Skin Issues (Surrounding skin other, specify:)

responseOptions.responseText: Induration > 4 cm in any area around wound
Non-pitting edema extends > 4 cm around wound
Pitting edema extends < 4 cm around wound
Crepitus and/or pitting edema extends > 4 cm around wound
